evaluations.transcript:
Speaker 0: This call is being recorded.
Speaker 1: Thank you for holding, bye. You're speaking to Alan. How can I help?
Speaker 0: Hi, Alan. This is Jean Hodge that you just sent a quote, uh, about-
Speaker 1: Hey, Jean
Speaker 0: - flights to Vienna.
Speaker 1: Yeah.
Speaker 0: I'm sorry, I was on the phone when you called earlier.
Speaker 1: It's all right. Uh, Jean, uh, just a quick question. Have you ever contacted us before?
Speaker 0: Nope.
Speaker 1: All right. Now, Jean, uh, you did mention that, uh, trip is slightly changed?
Speaker 0: Yes.
Speaker 1: So we are still going to Vienna, uh, around twenty-fifth of July-
Speaker 0: Correct.
Speaker 1: - but, uh, we would like to be, uh, coming back out of Rome. And, uh-
Speaker 0: Correct.
Speaker 1: So earlier you were le-- you were coming back in just a week out of Vienna. So now, uh, h- the total trip, like the total duration, how much that would be, including Rome?
Speaker 0: Um, so we're gonna spend, like, four nights in Austria-
Speaker 1: Okay.
Speaker 0: - and then I would like to spend three nights in Rome.
Speaker 1: I see. So the trip is still around, like, seven, eight days, but, uh, like, you would like to divide the Vienna trip into two?
Speaker 0: Right. Now, the reason I'm not asking you about accommodations in Austria-
Speaker 1: Uh-huh.
Speaker 0: - is they have, they have a place... My son is going to a hockey camp.
Speaker 1: I see.
Speaker 0: And they have a place for us to stay through the hockey camp so that we don't have to rent a car or anything, that we- he can just walk to the ice rink.
Speaker 1: Like, is it a professional thing or is it, uh, like, just training thing-
Speaker 0: No
Speaker 1: - which he's going for?
Speaker 0: It's, it's, it's called hobby hockey.
Speaker 1: Oh, okay.
Speaker 0: But it is for adults. My son is twenty-four, and-
Speaker 1: I see.
Speaker 0: - it's gonna look really good, it's gonna look really good on his US resume because he's graduating-
Speaker 1: Uh-huh.
Speaker 0: - not this year, but he's graduating in twenty twenty-six, and while he goes for his master's degree, he'd like to find a job as a coach somewhere.
Speaker 1: Oh, okay. I see. So this is a part of it. So all right, uh, I mean, uh, we, there are total of three passengers, three adults, right?
Speaker 0: Correct.
Speaker 1: Okay. So, uh, give me one second.
Speaker 0: I'm so excited. I can, I can't even sit still. [laughing]
Speaker 1: I, I, I could, I, I could actually hear that in your email. Uh, again-
Speaker 0: [laughing]
Speaker 1: - I knew that, how excited you were.
Speaker 0: Well- [laughing]
Speaker 1: So that's why I wanted to know that what exactly it is.
Speaker 0: Right. Yeah.
Speaker 1: So again, it's something which is actually, uh, I think would come in handy, uh, in future-
Speaker 0: Yes
Speaker 1: - if he's looking for a career in, uh, hockey.
Speaker 0: Yep. I mean, we, we knew from the start he was never gonna play professionally, and he may surprise us-
Speaker 1: Uh-huh.
Speaker 0: - yet by playing minor league, 'cause he, he's good, but you know, he's not gonna be in the NHL.
Speaker 1: Okay.
Speaker 0: Um, so coaching-
Speaker 1: All right
Speaker 0: - is a way for him to stay close to the sport he loves.
Speaker 1: Yes.
Speaker 0: He's been skating since he was four.
Speaker 1: Oh, wow! So he lo... Again, this is what he loves, and, uh, again, you know, even though he cannot- he's not professionally playing, but at least he can coach, uh, if he's good at it. At least the knowledge is there.
Speaker 0: Right.
Speaker 1: Right? So-
Speaker 0: Right
Speaker 1: ... that can come in handy. All right, uh, I mean, uh, so the deal which was there, uh, usually, you know, it is, uh, from the same country. If, uh, if it's the same country that... So the market is about thirty-five, thirty-six hundred. The deal which I sent you was, was about twenty-seven hundred, and, uh, that is, like, again, uh, it's a deal which we have with our consolidator. Uh, but it's like, uh, to, for you, giving an example, if it's, we are going to Frankfurt, that is in Germany, and we are coming back off Munich, it's still fine because both airports are in Germany, and that, that's how it prices.
Speaker 0: Oh, okay.
Speaker 1: But, uh, coming out of Rome, uh, it... I'll, I'll, I'll have a look again. I'll send it to my experts. I wanted to know, uh, how flexible are we with the dates?
Speaker 0: Um, not at all on Austria. That, because that-
Speaker 1: So-
Speaker 0: - those are the dates of the...
Speaker 1: So you have to be there by the twenty-sixth?
Speaker 0: Whatever I sent you, yes.
Speaker 1: Yeah, that is twenty-sixth. So, uh, so you, you sent me twenty-fifth of July, Friday, leaving. So you would be arriving there on Saturday, which is the twenty-sixth.
Speaker 0: Um-
Speaker 1: Okay?
Speaker 0: ... actually, no, no, no. Could we leave on Thursday and get there Friday?
Speaker 1: Okay, so leaving on the twenty-fourth-
Speaker 0: Correct.
Speaker 1: - then, and arriving on the, on Friday?
Speaker 0: Yeah.
Speaker 1: Now, coming back out of Rome, uh, so should I count Friday night as well in Austria?
Speaker 0: Yes. I, I-
Speaker 1: Okay, so Friday night.
Speaker 0: I, I completely spaced out.
Speaker 1: Okay.
Speaker 0: They're having a reception and an orientation on Friday night in Austria, so we need to be there for that.
Speaker 1: Okay. So Friday, uh, Saturday, Sunday and Monday, you would like to be in Vienna?
Speaker 0: Correct.
Speaker 1: Then on Tuesday, uh, let's say in the morning, you would like to take a flight from Austria to Rome, and then-
Speaker 0: Right
Speaker 1: ... uh, stay Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday in Rome and, uh, leave there from, on Friday?
Speaker 0: Correct.
Speaker 1: So that is-
Speaker 0: I cannot tell...
Speaker 1: Go ahead.
Speaker 0: This is the best news I have gotten since they told me I was cancer-free twenty years ago.
Speaker 1: Oh, wow!
Speaker 0: This is how exciting this is. [laughing]
Speaker 1: You beat cancer, too?
Speaker 0: Yep.
Speaker 1: ... Wow! Really glad that she did.
Speaker 0: It, it is very weird that, Alan, that, uh, one night we, you know, hockey parents like to sit around in the hotel bar and drink, and one night-
Speaker 1: Uh-huh.
Speaker 0: -we discovered that the rate of breast cancer among hockey mothers is something like three times the average breast cancer rate.
Speaker 1: Oh.
Speaker 0: So there must be something about our sons seeing us beat this disease that turns them into hockey players.
Speaker 1: Oh. Or could it be something that, you know, you stay, uh, close to the ice so much? [laughing] Did you ever think about that?
Speaker 0: Uh, no, it, it's in my family. My mother had it, my sister had it-
Speaker 1: Oh.
Speaker 0: -I had it, you know, so this was not unexpected.
Speaker 1: I see.
Speaker 0: What was unexpected is that we were all so young.
Speaker 1: I understand. Now, uh, see, the th-thing, Jean, is, uh, that if we are going to Lex... From out of Lexington, is there any other airport that you can leave out of, apart, apart from Lexington?
Speaker 0: Um, we could leave out of Cincinnati, if that is helpful.
Speaker 1: Uh, no, that might not help. But again, just to give you an idea, uh, if we go- leave out of, uh, Lexington to Vienna, uh, coming back Rome, out of, uh, Rome to Lexington, the market is, uh, if we are taking [PHONE] stops, it becomes-
Speaker 0: Yep.
Speaker 1: -about thirty-[PHONE], thirty-[PHONE] hundred. Uh, [PHONE] stops would be-
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: -[PHONE] in, uh, US and the other [PHONE] would be in London somewhere, or maybe in Germany or something like that. Okay?
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: Uh, I'm gonna try and, I'm gonna try and see that, you know, if I can actually make it [PHONE] stop and slightly-
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: -better. That's my job, right?
Speaker 0: Well, yep, and, and here's the thing: so you know-
Speaker 1: Uh-huh.
Speaker 0: -and you feel good about yourself and what you quote me. I am using money that I have squirreled away for years, so it sort of-
Speaker 1: Uh-huh.
Speaker 0: -doesn't matter what you tell me. I trust you to get me the best deal, I'm gonna pay it.
Speaker 1: Now, you actually, you know, uh, kind of gave me an, uh, like, reason to actually do be- like, even better and to save that money-
Speaker 0: [laughing]
Speaker 1: -which you actually saved for all those years. So I'll, I'll-
Speaker 0: Yeah.
Speaker 1: -do my best, see. All right?
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: And, uh-
Speaker 0: Well, that's-
Speaker 1: -uh, I'll try and, I'll try and make it more comfortable so that... Now, again, two stops is actually okay because you're leaving out of Lexington.
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: Most of the flights are two stops, but there are a few one stops, too. Uh, I'm gonna try and see what I can do with one stop, and, uh-
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: -if nothing, at least, you know, I'll try and at least get better price. Okay?
Speaker 0: Okay. No, that, that's fine.
Speaker 1: Uh, do-
Speaker 0: And I-
Speaker 1: Should I, should I add the flight between Austria and Rome as well? Uh, like, I mean that-
Speaker 0: Yes.
Speaker 1: -you can take an economy, too, because that's a short flight. Would that okay?
Speaker 0: Yep.
Speaker 1: Would that be okay?
Speaker 0: Yep.
Speaker 1: Perfect.
Speaker 0: Hey, listen-
Speaker 1: Again, thank you, Jean.
Speaker 0: But-
Speaker 1: Sorry, go ahead.
Speaker 0: Okay. All right, have a great day.
Speaker 1: Sorry, go ahead. You were... No, no, you were saying something. Please, uh, you were saying something.
Speaker 0: I, I was gonna say, if you knew the fleabag hotels we've stayed in over the years, [laughing] an economy seat-
Speaker 1: Uh-huh.
Speaker 0: -is not gonna kill any of us.
Speaker 1: Oh, okay.
Speaker 0: One, one hotel was so bad we slept in our clothes.
Speaker 1: Oh.
Speaker 0: [laughing]
Speaker 1: Wow! That's how, that's how you save so, so, like, that money and, uh, that's why-
Speaker 0: Right.
Speaker 1: -I believe... Uh, like, is it the first time you're traveling in business, or you have done this before as well?
Speaker 0: Uh, now, I have been to Germany and Rome. My husband and my son have not.
Speaker 1: Oh, I see. So you have traveled, but they, they have not?
Speaker 0: Correct. I used to work for Procter and Gamble, and my work sent me.
Speaker 1: Wow.
Speaker 0: But we've all got updated passports, and we're ready to go.
Speaker 1: Perfect. All right. I'll, I'll make sure that now I... Because, again, the travel is just in couple of months, and it's summertime. A lot of travel-
Speaker 0: Right
Speaker 1: -happening to Europe.
Speaker 0: Yep.
Speaker 1: So I'll make sure that, you know, we get it, this done as soon as possible, and then, uh, again, thank you so much for calling back. Uh, I'll, uh, again, the number I'm, uh, you're calling from, it's your cell phone, right?
Speaker 0: Correct.
Speaker 1: I'll text you the moment-
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: -you know, I send you any quotes. Okay?
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: And you have a look, and let me know.
Speaker 0: Okay, will do.
Speaker 1: Thanks, Jean. Take care. Bye-bye.
Speaker 0: All right. You, too.
Speaker 1: Talk to you soon.
Speaker 0: Bye-bye. Okay.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Agent: Thank you for holding, bye. You're speaking to Alan. How can I help?

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk.

advisor_positioning.evidence: The agent did not position themselves as a 'Senior Travel Expert' or similar.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Agent: Uh, Jean, uh, just a quick question. Have you ever contacted us before?

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Agent: So we are still going to Vienna, uh, around twenty-fifth of July- Client: Correct.

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: Agent: Like, is it a professional thing or is it, uh, like, just training thing- Client: No Agent: -which he's going for?

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: Agent: I wanted to know, uh, how flexible are we with the dates?

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about comfort or medical needs such as lie-flat beds or mobility requirements.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the 'experience' or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade.

pain_point_identification.evidence: The agent did not ask which airlines the client wants to avoid and why.

budget_inquiry.evidence: Client: I trust you to get me the best deal, I'm gonna pay it.

recap_summary.evidence: Agent: So Friday, uh, Saturday, Sunday and Monday, you would like to be in Vienna? Client: Correct.

contact_branding.evidence: The agent did not ask the client to save their number as 'Agent Name - Travel.'

expert_delay.evidence: The agent did not explain that they need 60-90 minutes to access private fares.

urgency_scarcity.evidence: Agent: I'll make sure that now I... Because, again, the travel is just in couple of months, and it's summertime. A lot of travel- Client: Right Agent: -happening to Europe.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for the follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer a best fare guarantee.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Agent: Uh, again, the number I'm, uh, you're calling from, it's your cell phone, right? Client: Correct.

active_listening.evidence: Agent: Oh, wow! So he lo... Again, this is what he loves, and, uh, again, you know, even though he cannot- he's not professionally playing, but at least he can coach, uh, if he's good at it.

consultative_expertise.evidence: Agent: Most of the flights are two stops, but there are a few one stops, too. Uh, I'm gonna try and see what I can do with one stop.

positive_tone.evidence: Agent: Now, you actually, you know, uh, kind of gave me an, uh, like, reason to actually do be- like, even better and to save that money- Client: [laughing]

authenticity.evidence: Agent: Wow! Really glad that she did.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent matched the client's conversational pace and tone throughout the call.

answers_questions.evidence: Agent: Should I add the flight between Austria and Rome as well? Uh, like, I mean that- Client: Yes.

call_leadership.evidence: Agent: I'll text you the moment- Client: Okay. Agent: -you know, I send you any quotes. Okay?

accurate_information.evidence: Agent: So you have to be there by the twenty-sixth? Client: Whatever I sent you, yes.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated strong active listening and maintained a positive tone throughout the call. However, they missed several key opportunities to position themselves as an expert and create urgency for a follow-up. The call lacked structured closing techniques, such as setting a hard appointment or offering a fare guarantee.
strengths: Strong active listening and engagement with the client.
Positive and enthusiastic tone maintained throughout the call.
Accurate confirmation of flight details and client preferences.
improvements: Incorporate advisor positioning to establish authority and expertise.
Set a hard appointment for the follow-up call to ensure client commitment.
Offer a fare guarantee to build trust and urgency.

evaluations.transcript:
Speaker 0: Hello. Hi, am I speaking with Angela?
Speaker 1: Yes, you are.
Speaker 0: Hi, Angela. My name is Alan. I'm calling from Buy Business Class. You put in a request that you're planning a trip to Barcelona?
Speaker 1: Yes.
Speaker 0: Next-
Speaker 1: Yes.
Speaker 0: All right. Uh, are you still looking for the tickets?
Speaker 1: Yes.
Speaker 0: Perfect. Angela, have you ever contacted us before?
Speaker 1: No.
Speaker 0: All right.
Speaker 1: And which company is this?
Speaker 0: Buy Business Class.
Speaker 1: Okay.
Speaker 0: All right. Uh, so I do- did receive your request that you're planning a trip from Tampa to Barcelona, leaving July twelfth, returning back on August fourth. Uh, so I was trying to reach you because, uh, you know, I wanted to ask you a few questions so that accordingly, I can send you a few options rather than sending you some random stuff. Right?
Speaker 1: Okay.
Speaker 0: So, uh, first of all, uh, is this a Europe trip or is there a cruise in- involved in this?
Speaker 1: There's a couple of cruises involved.
Speaker 0: I see. And, uh, in the end, uh, you end up in Barcelona or somewhere else in Europe?
Speaker 1: In Barcelona.
Speaker 0: Okay.
Speaker 1: To and from.
Speaker 0: I see. So, uh, now, uh, the date which you mentioned, uh, July twelfth, uh, is it flexible slightly, like plus minus one, a couple of days?
Speaker 1: Yeah, flexible.
Speaker 0: When does the-
Speaker 1: Unflexible.
Speaker 0: When does the first cruise leave?
Speaker 1: No, the eighteenth.
Speaker 0: Oh, I see.
Speaker 1: The eighteenth.
Speaker 0: All right. So you want to spend some time in Barcelona as well, and on the way back-
Speaker 1: Correct.
Speaker 0: On the way back, is it arriving on the fourth itself?
Speaker 1: The, uh, cruise ship arrives on the third.
Speaker 0: I see.
Speaker 1: I just didn't know what time flights leave and do I have enough time to get to the airport. So I just picked the fourth, but I'd rather fly out on the third.
Speaker 0: I'll tell you about the cruises, how they work. Uh, actually, the cruise will, uh, you know, uh, I think it will disembark around, um, around seven, eight. That's what the cruise times are mostly. And it will take you couple of hours to actually check out, uh, completely with the luggage and everything. And, uh, and then, um, if you have to take the flight on the same day, it has to be after one PM at least, so that you don't miss it.
Speaker 1: Okay.
Speaker 0: Uh, so-
Speaker 1: Right.
Speaker 0: Because, again, we do it all the time, so I know, uh, those things, how it works.
Speaker 1: Right.
Speaker 0: So, uh, then again, if-
Speaker 1: And the other thing, Alan-
Speaker 0: Right
Speaker 1: ... is that we're flexible on is I don't have to leave out of Tampa. I'm just looking for a direct business class, either from Miami, Tampa, either one, or I'm going to have to drive to.
Speaker 0: I think... Okay, well, so which one is the closest? Where, where exactly you're in Florida?
Speaker 1: I'm in Southwest Florida, so both are going to be the same, basically, difference.
Speaker 0: I see.
Speaker 1: I'm just more familiar with the Tampa Airport than the Miami Airport.
Speaker 0: Oh, I see. Now, uh-
Speaker 1: If they don't have a direct business class and prices will dictate one or the other.
Speaker 0: Of course. Uh, now, give me one second. Now, you mentioned that you are looking for a nonstop?
Speaker 1: Correct, business class.
Speaker 0: And I'll tell you, I'm-- I think, like, Tampa doesn't have a nonstop flight to-
Speaker 1: Okay. So Miami
Speaker 0: ... Barcelona. Uh, for su- I'm just making sure of it. I'm, like, I'm at least ninety percent sure they do- don't, uh... I don't know whether they start anything with next year. [coughs] Sorry about that. Uh, so even next year, they don't have a nonstop, so not for now, at least. And, uh, Miami, uh, they definitely have a nonstop. Now, if we are looking for a nonstop, we are then only stuck with one airline, which is American.
Speaker 1: Yep.
Speaker 0: There's-
Speaker 1: Okay
Speaker 0: ... there's no other airlines apart from that. Now-
Speaker 1: Okay
Speaker 0: ... uh, would Orlando be an option, too?
Speaker 1: That's, that's like another hour drive for me.
Speaker 0: Oh, let, let, let's forget it then. Because, uh... All right, then Miami it is then. Okay?
Speaker 1: Yep.
Speaker 0: Now, uh, Miami to Barcelona, coming back from Barcelona to Miami, uh, two passengers, and we are looking for only business class, correct?
Speaker 1: Correct.
Speaker 0: Got it. All right, uh, I think I, I already have all the requirements, uh, you know, to start working on this. Now, uh, again, uh, you already know my name, uh, Alan. I will be the one working with you on this. I, I will be the only one, uh, from Buy Business Class working on this, and I have-
Speaker 1: Okay
Speaker 0: ... sent you an email on your email address. Could you just quickly check and see if you got that?
Speaker 1: Mm-hmm. Yep.
Speaker 0: Perfect.
Speaker 1: Alan Mondel.
Speaker 0: Yes. And, uh, the number I'm calling on, is, is it your cell phone?
Speaker 1: Yes.
Speaker 0: Now, um, just the last question, uh, I would not ask you about the price which you're looking for. I'll just send you the best deals there are. Uh, I would ask you that, how soon are we looking to finalize, uh, the tickets?
Speaker 1: Uh, hopefully by tomorrow.
Speaker 0: Perfect. Amazing. So I'll, I'll send you a few options, and then, uh, can I call you back on the same number? So again, this, the number I'm calling you from right now, the seven O eight number, this is my direct line, okay? I'll be calling you back-
Speaker 1: Okay
Speaker 0: ... on from the same number itself.... So if you want, you can save it or-
Speaker 1: I'll put you in my contacts.
Speaker 0: That is perfect. So that you know it's me, and, uh, I will call you the moment I have all the deals, uh, you know, shut it down, and I will send them to you via email so you can check all the details of the flight numbers to the layover. Sorry, there won't be any layovers, though, uh, to the travel duration and everything. Okay?
Speaker 1: Right.
Speaker 0: And then, uh, we can discuss again whether it's the price or the timing of the flight. If you w- would like any changes, you can let me know so that I can make those changes.
Speaker 1: Right. Right. And do you only handle airlines or do you also handle, uh, hotels?
Speaker 0: Hotels, yes, we can, uh, uh, help you with the hotels, too. Cruises, not so much, but with the hotels-
Speaker 1: Okay. The cruises are already shored up.
Speaker 0: Oh, that's-
Speaker 1: I just know that I'm... Right. So Barcelona, um, if we come in on the twelfth, which again, I'm flexible, um, I'm gonna need to stay there until the eighteenth.
Speaker 0: Yes. So again-
Speaker 1: And then-
Speaker 0: ... let's find out the let's figure out the dates of the flights, which are-
Speaker 1: Yeah
Speaker 0: ... which gives you the best rate and, uh, again, the best timings and whatever. And then we can, you know, think, start working on the hotels because once we are, once we are aware that know when we are arriving there, then we know how many days we are staying in the hotel. And I will obviously, uh, for that as well, I would need your preference at what kind of hotels you require. Like, you know, some people like to stay, uh, like, the cheapest room there are, and some people like to stay in a, like, luxury room, like five star, four star. Again, those all the questions I will be asking you once we sh, you know, uh, like, complete the flights. All right?
Speaker 1: Okay, sounds good. Yep.
Speaker 0: Perfect. Thank you, thank you, Miss Black. I'll get back to you soon. Take care.
Speaker 1: All right. Thank you, Alan. Bye-bye.
Speaker 0: Bye-bye.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Agent: Hi, Angela. My name is Alan. I'm calling from Buy Business Class. You put in a request that you're planning a trip to Barcelona?

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk or seek permission to proceed with the call.

advisor_positioning.evidence: The agent introduced themselves by name but did not position themselves as a 'Senior Travel Expert' or similar.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Agent: Perfect. Angela, have you ever contacted us before?

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Agent: Uh, so I do- did receive your request that you're planning a trip from Tampa to Barcelona, leaving July twelfth, returning back on August fourth.

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: Agent: So, uh, first of all, uh, is this a Europe trip or is there a cruise in- involved in this?

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: Agent: I see. So, uh, now, uh, the date which you mentioned, uh, July twelfth, uh, is it flexible slightly, like plus minus one, a couple of days?

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about specific comfort or medical needs such as lie-flat beds or mobility requirements.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the 'experience' (food/wine/service) or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade.

pain_point_identification.evidence: The agent did not ask which airlines the client wants to avoid and why.

budget_inquiry.evidence: Agent: Now, um, just the last question, uh, I would not ask you about the price which you're looking for. I'll just send you the best deals there are.

recap_summary.evidence: Agent: Now, uh, Miami to Barcelona, coming back from Barcelona to Miami, uh, two passengers, and we are looking for only business class, correct?

contact_branding.evidence: Agent: So again, this, the number I'm calling you from right now, the seven O eight number, this is my direct line, okay? I'll be calling you back-

expert_delay.evidence: The agent did not explain that they need 60-90 minutes to access 'private, unpublished fares.'

urgency_scarcity.evidence: The agent did not mention that fares and availability change quickly to encourage a faster decision.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for the follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer a best fare guarantee or mention the ability to beat any prices.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Agent: ... Could you just quickly check and see if you got that? Client: Mm-hmm. Yep.

active_listening.evidence: The agent frequently acknowledged the client's responses and adjusted questions accordingly.

consultative_expertise.evidence: Agent: Because, again, we do it all the time, so I know, uh, those things, how it works.

positive_tone.evidence: The agent maintained an upbeat and positive tone throughout the call.

authenticity.evidence: The agent responded appropriately to the client's questions and concerns.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent matched the client's pace and communication style effectively.

answers_questions.evidence: Agent: Hotels, yes, we can, uh, uh, help you with the hotels, too. Cruises, not so much, but with the hotels-

call_leadership.evidence: The agent guided the conversation but did not fully take control by setting a follow-up appointment.

accurate_information.evidence: Agent: I'll tell you about the cruises, how they work. Uh, actually, the cruise will, uh, you know, uh, I think it will disembark around, um, around seven, eight.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated strong active listening and consultative expertise, ensuring the client felt heard and understood. However, there were missed opportunities to create urgency, position themselves as an expert, and set a hard follow-up appointment, which could improve the likelihood of closing the sale.
strengths: Strong active listening and acknowledgment of client responses.
Demonstrated consultative expertise and familiarity with travel logistics.
Maintained a positive and professional tone throughout the call.
improvements: Ask for permission to proceed with the call to establish rapport.
Position yourself as a Senior Travel Expert to build credibility.
Create urgency by emphasizing fare volatility and set a specific follow-up time.

However, they missed several opportunities to establish authority, create urgency, and set up a follow-up call effectively. The call lacked psychological framing and strategic closing techniques.
strengths: Active listening and matching the client's pace.
Accurate information about flight options and pricing.
Positive and authentic tone throughout the call.
improvements: Establish authority by positioning as a travel expert and using a more enthusiastic tone.
Create urgency by emphasizing fare volatility and suggesting a follow-up time.
Probe for the client's dream outcome and budget to better tailor recommendations.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Agent: Yeah, hi. Uh, my name is Ma- Hello?

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk.

advisor_positioning.evidence: Agent: Yeah, my name is Mahi. I'm calling you from BuyBusiness Class, a travel agency.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Agent: Uh, a quick thing to know, have you ever contacted us before?

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Agent: Could you please help me with the flight information? What are you looking for? Like, from where to where you're traveling, what are the dates?

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about the occasion or the client's dominant buying motive.

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: The agent did not suggest shifting dates or using nearby airports.

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about comfort or medical needs.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the experience or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade.

pain_point_identification.evidence: The agent did not ask which airlines the client wants to avoid and why.

budget_inquiry.evidence: Agent: Um, business class would at least cost you four thousand dollars, ma'am.

recap_summary.evidence: The agent did not summarize the client's needs or priorities.

contact_branding.evidence: The agent did not tell the client to save their number as 'Agent Name - Travel.'

expert_delay.evidence: The agent did not explain the need for time to access private fares.

urgency_scarcity.evidence: The agent did not mention that fares and availability change quickly.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for a follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer a best fare guarantee or the ability to beat prices.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: The agent did not confirm the client's contact information.

active_listening.evidence: Agent: Okay. Mon- Montreal? Client: M-O-N-T-R-O-S-E.

consultative_expertise.evidence: Agent: That would be around twenty-five hundred dollars with the American Airlines. A flight from, uh, Montrose to Dallas, Dallas to London, London to Barcelona.

positive_tone.evidence: The agent maintained a neutral tone but lacked consistent positivity.

authenticity.evidence: The agent responded appropriately to the client's questions and provided relevant information.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent matched the client's pace during the conversation.

answers_questions.evidence: Agent: Let me check that, too.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent led the conversation but did not fully control the flow or set clear next steps.

accurate_information.evidence: Agent: That would be around twenty-five hundred dollars with the American Airlines. A flight from, uh, Montrose to Dallas, Dallas to London, London to Barcelona.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated basic competence in confirming flight details and providing accurate information but lacked enthusiasm and failed to establish themselves as an expert. Key elements like psychological framing, urgency, and follow-up planning were missing, which reduced the effectiveness of the call.
strengths: Confirmed flight details accurately.
Provided clear and accurate pricing information.
Matched the client's pace and responded authentically.
improvements: Adopt a more enthusiastic and confident tone to establish authority.
Incorporate psychological framing techniques, such as discovering the client's dominant buying motive and testing for flexibility.
Set clear follow-up steps, including a hard appointment and contact branding.
critical_gaps: MULTI_AGENT: 2 agents detected in call — Ma, Mahi. QA score may mix multiple agents' performance.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Agent: Yeah, my name is Matt Kern. I'm looking, um, on the website. I'm looking for flights, uh, from Atlanta, Pensacola or Atlanta to Barcelona.

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk at any point during the call.

advisor_positioning.evidence: The agent did not position themselves as a 'Senior Travel Expert' or similar.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company, but this is not critical for this call type.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Client: Sure. Before we proceed further, just a quick question. Have you ever contacted us before? Agent: No.

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Client: So you would like to fly from Atlanta to Barcelona, right? Agent: Or Pensacola, depending on the timing of the flight.

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: Agent: I'd like to do business class. I think it's a long, I think it's a long flight to go, um, if I, if it's affordable.

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: Client: The traveling dates are fully fixed, or can it be plus one day, minus one day also, if we get a good difference in the prices? Agent: Um, not on the way back. I've gotta be, I've gotta be back by... I have to be back by the third, so.

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: Agent: I'd like to do business class. I think it's a long, I think it's a long flight to go, um, if I, if it's affordable.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the 'experience' or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade.

pain_point_identification.evidence: The agent did not ask which airlines the client wants to avoid and why.

budget_inquiry.evidence: Client: Okay, just to confirm, Matthew, as you mentioned, that you don't want to spend more on the flight ticket.

recap_summary.evidence: The agent did not summarize the 'Why,' 'The Plan,' and 'The Priority' back to the client.

contact_branding.evidence: The agent did not ask the client to save their number as 'Agent Name - Travel.'

expert_delay.evidence: Client: Please hold the line. Let me check what all are the best options we can get.

urgency_scarcity.evidence: Client: And in this one, we are just getting a, like, since this is a discounted fare, so we have only three seats available at this price.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for a follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer a best fare guarantee or the ability to beat any prices.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Client: And the number you are calling, this is the best number of yours to reach you back, right? Agent: This, this is the best nu- This, this is the best number. Correct.

active_listening.evidence: The agent occasionally interrupted the client and did not always match their pace.

consultative_expertise.evidence: Agent: Do you know what, w- what's, what's the main difference between business class and premium economy?

positive_tone.evidence: The agent's tone was neutral and lacked consistent positivity.

authenticity.evidence: Agent: Okay, so now you can, you can reply and send-- hopefully send it back to me, and I'll get it.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent's pace was inconsistent and did not always match the client's.

answers_questions.evidence: Agent: Yes. So in business class you will get fully lie-flat seat, so that you can lay down, but in premium economy you will get just a recliner.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent occasionally lost control of the call, especially during silences and email troubleshooting.

accurate_information.evidence: Agent: So in business class you will get fully lie-flat seat, so that you can lay down, but in premium economy you will get just a recliner.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated some strengths in confirming flight details and addressing the client's budget concerns. However, the call lacked enthusiasm, clear leadership, and strategic framing to build urgency or secure a follow-up. The agent also struggled with maintaining a consistent pace and tone.
strengths: Confirmed flight details and client preferences effectively.
Addressed budget concerns and provided clear fare options.
Provided accurate information about flight classes and features.
improvements: Adopt a more enthusiastic and confident tone to establish authority.
Proactively summarize the client's needs and set expectations for follow-up.
Improve call leadership by minimizing silences and maintaining control of the conversation.
critical_gaps: The agent did not position themselves as an expert or create urgency for a follow-up.
The agent struggled with email troubleshooting, which delayed the process.

answers_questions.evidence: The agent answered all of the client's questions about the protection plan and flight options.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent guided the client through the call, confirming details and setting expectations for the follow-up.

accurate_information.evidence: The agent provided accurate information about the protection plan and the nature of private deals.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated strong communication skills, maintained a positive tone, and provided detailed information about the protection plan and flight options. However, there were missed opportunities to gather more information about the client's dream outcome, budget, and marketing source. The follow-up process could have been more structured with a specific appointment time.
strengths: Maintained a positive and enthusiastic tone throughout the call.
Demonstrated consultative expertise by explaining the protection plan in detail.
Effectively probed for flexibility and confirmed flight details.
improvements: Ask about the client's dream outcome or occasion for travel to better understand their priorities.
Confirm the client's contact information, including email and phone number.
Set a specific time for the follow-up call to ensure clarity and commitment.

accurate_information.evidence: Agent: So the flight would be, yeah, so we, we leave on the twelfth, we get in the thirteenth, and then the additional one would be going to Lisbon on the nineteenth.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated some effort in providing information and answering questions but lacked structure and key elements of a strong first call. Critical areas such as permission checking, advisor positioning, and urgency creation were missing, which impacted the overall effectiveness of the call.
strengths: Provided accurate information about the flight details.
Encouraged the client to save their contact information.
Answered client questions effectively.
improvements: Ask for permission to proceed with the call to ensure client availability.
Position yourself as a travel expert to build credibility.
Create urgency by emphasizing fare and availability changes.

budget_inquiry.evidence: Agent: And what was the price for both the itinerary, sir, that you got?

contact_branding.evidence: The agent did not tell the client to save their number as 'Agent Name - Travel.'

expert_delay.evidence: Agent: Do me a favor, sir. I do not want, uh, you to wait. I would check with the expert once.

urgency_scarcity.evidence: The agent did not mention that fares and availability change quickly.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for the follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer the best fare guarantee or the ability to beat any prices.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Agent: And in case we get disconnected, is this the best number to ring you back, sir?

consultative_expertise.evidence: Agent: Because it's very strange. Like, if you are leaving on the same date, the prices, uh, with the KLM, uh, it's per passenger, like, um, a combination of both the trip is showing me, like, ninety-four hundred, sir, per passenger.

positive_tone.evidence: The agent maintained a neutral tone but did not consistently use upbeat or positive language.

authenticity.evidence: The agent responded appropriately to the client's questions and comments.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent matched the client's pace and allowed them to think and respond.

answers_questions.evidence: The agent answered the client's questions and provided options.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent led parts of the conversation but did not fully take control of the call.

accurate_information.evidence: The agent provided accurate information about flight options and pricing.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated good active listening and confirmed key details effectively. However, they missed opportunities to position themselves as an expert, explore the client's dream outcome, and create urgency or scarcity. The call lacked a strong closing strategy to secure the follow-up.
strengths: Effective confirmation of flight details.
Good active listening and pace matching.
Accurate information provided about flight options.
improvements: Position yourself as a travel expert to build credibility.
Ask about the client's dream outcome and dominant buying motive.
Create urgency and set a specific time for the follow-up call.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Agent: Yeah, hi, Max. This is Alice from Buy Business Class. I'm calling regarding your flight ticket. Are you looking for a flight reservation?

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk or seek permission to proceed with the call.

advisor_positioning.evidence: The agent introduced themselves as 'Alice from Buy Business Class' but did not position themselves as a 'Senior Travel Expert' or similar.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Agent: Okay. Before we proceed, I would like to ask you, like, have you ever contacted to Buy Business Class?

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Agent: Okay, sir, got it. So you just, uh, basically wanna go from Minneapolis, correct?

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about the occasion or the client's dominant buying motive.

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: Agent: So your dates are a little flex, like one day here and there. Only more-- I mean, the main important- Client: Yeah, but-

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: Agent: Any airline will work, right? You said that you've been on Lufthansa Airline also, uh, Royal Jordanian Airline also, correct?

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the experience or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade for a specific price.

pain_point_identification.evidence: Client: I mean, I travel with everybody. They're, they're, they're all terrible. Agent: Oh, okay. [chuckles]

budget_inquiry.evidence: The agent did not ask about the client's budget or price expectations.

recap_summary.evidence: Agent: Okay. I have all the seats for you, okay? You prefer the window seat. It's available here.

contact_branding.evidence: Agent: Please save my number. Mm-hmm.

expert_delay.evidence: The agent did not explain that they need 60-90 minutes to access private, unpublished fares.

urgency_scarcity.evidence: The agent did not mention that fares and availability change quickly.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for a follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer a best fare guarantee or mention beating any prices.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Agent: Yeah, I have sent you in your email by Alice Dante. Could you check once?

active_listening.evidence: Agent: Got it, sir. No problem. So let me check the best available flight, okay?

consultative_expertise.evidence: Agent: Once the flight is booked, I will assign the seat selection. If you have any frequent flyer and all, you can help me out that also, I'll update it.

positive_tone.evidence: Agent maintained a positive tone throughout the call, e.g., 'No problem. In case, you can just do no protection because you need to select that also to submit the form.'

authenticity.evidence: Agent: Thank you very much for tipping me up, sir. Means lot, means a lot to me.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent matched the client's pace, especially when assisting with the form submission.

answers_questions.evidence: Agent: Yes, window seats I can assign. Once the flight is booked, I will assign the seat selection.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent guided the client through the booking process but did not fully take control of the call.

accurate_information.evidence: The agent provided flight details but did not clearly explain the advertised offer or discounts.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated good active listening and maintained a positive tone throughout the call. However, there were missed opportunities to position themselves as an expert, create urgency, and fully explore the client's needs and preferences. The call lacked a structured approach to closing and follow-up planning.
strengths: Maintained a positive and authentic tone.
Demonstrated active listening and matched the client's pace.
Guided the client through the booking process effectively.
improvements: Position themselves as a senior travel expert to build credibility.
Create urgency by emphasizing fare volatility and availability.
Ask about the client's dream outcome and budget expectations.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Agent: Yeah. Hi, Forrest. This is Emma from Buy Business Class. I'm sorry I missed your call.

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk.

advisor_positioning.evidence: The agent did not position themselves as a 'Senior Travel Expert' or similar.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Agent: Yeah, but that's okay. I was working on your request, and I found some options for you.

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Agent: Okay. So I'm getting a deal with United and Swiss Airline. This is leaving from San Francisco, nineteen May. Departure, two forty PM.

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about the occasion or the client's dominant buying motive.

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: Agent: So you can give me an idea how many days you would like to stay in Amsterdam.

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: Agent: Yes, you'll get fully lie-flat seat.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the experience or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade.

pain_point_identification.evidence: The agent did not ask which airlines the client wants to avoid and why.

budget_inquiry.evidence: The agent did not ask about the client's budget or price expectations.

recap_summary.evidence: Agent: Perfect. You'll see an option at the bottom side to book now, and that's our final booking page.

contact_branding.evidence: The agent did not ask the client to save their number as 'Agent Name - Travel.'

expert_delay.evidence: The agent did not explain needing time to access private fares.

urgency_scarcity.evidence: Agent: Then let's go ahead and get this before we lose the seat availability.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for a follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer a best fare guarantee.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Agent: You have the miles number? You can give it to me, I will enter it in the reservation.

active_listening.evidence: Agent: Mm-hmm. Right. Correct.

consultative_expertise.evidence: Agent: Nowadays, like, all the, uh, airlines are offering fully lie-flat seat, except few like Icelandair.

positive_tone.evidence: Agent: That's really nice, and I'm sure you will also be a return customer.

authenticity.evidence: Agent: I'm working for the US-based company, and our head offices are in Chicago, but we have multiple locations.

pace_matching.evidence: Agent: Mm-hmm. Right. Correct.

answers_questions.evidence: Agent: You need to select both of the checkbox. One is about the charges, the other one is about terms and conditions.

call_leadership.evidence: Agent: Let me send you the option so that you can take a look.

accurate_information.evidence: Agent: This is we are getting, uh, both ways nonstop business class, except the flight Amsterdam to Zurich will be in economy.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated strong consultative expertise and provided accurate information, ensuring the client felt supported throughout the call. However, the agent missed opportunities to position themselves as an expert, explore the client's dream outcome, and establish a follow-up plan. Improvements in opening techniques and psychological framing would enhance the overall experience.
strengths: Strong consultative expertise and accurate information.
Positive and authentic tone throughout the call.
Effective active listening and pace matching.
improvements: Incorporate a permission-based opening and advisor positioning.
Explore the client's dream outcome and dominant buying motive.
Establish a clear follow-up plan with a specific time.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Thank you for calling Buy Business Class. This is Allen. How can I help?

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask 'Is it a good time to talk?' or a similar permission-based question.

advisor_positioning.evidence: The agent did not position themselves as a 'Senior Travel Expert' or similar.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Have you ever contacted us before?

confirm_flight_details.evidence: The agent confirmed the dates and destinations but did not ask what the client had already found online.

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about the occasion or the client's dominant buying motive.

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: And since we are only looking for nonstop out of LA or, uh, you know, one stop maximum out of San Diego, uh, there are not too many airlines, of course.

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about comfort or medical needs such as lie-flat beds or mobility requirements.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the 'experience' or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade.

pain_point_identification.evidence: The agent asked about airline preferences but did not explore airlines the client wants to avoid or why.

budget_inquiry.evidence: The agent did not ask about the client's budget or price expectations.

recap_summary.evidence: The agent did not summarize the 'Why,' 'The Plan,' and 'The Priority' back to the client.

contact_branding.evidence: The agent did not tell the client to save their number as 'Agent Name - Travel.'

expert_delay.evidence: I mean, uh, so because I just have to make sure that know whatever the deal which we can get, I have everything so that we, you know, when I call you back, I can offer you the best option.

urgency_scarcity.evidence: The agent did not mention that fares and availability change quickly.

hard_appointment.evidence: Can you give me about maybe, uh, half an hour?

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer the best fare guarantee or the ability to beat any prices.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Just to confirm, the email address I have for you is F as in Frederick, uh, V as in Victor, E-

active_listening.evidence: The agent listened to the client's preferences and confirmed details such as dates and airline preferences.

consultative_expertise.evidence: The agent displayed some expertise by discussing nonstop flights and airline alliances but did not fully guide the client with consultative advice.

positive_tone.evidence: The agent maintained a polite tone but lacked consistent enthusiasm or energy.

authenticity.evidence: The agent responded appropriately to the client's questions and preferences.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent matched the client's pace and allowed them to express their preferences without interruption.

answers_questions.evidence: The agent answered the client's questions about airline alliances and flight options.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent guided the client through the call but did not fully take control to establish next steps clearly.

accurate_information.evidence: The agent provided accurate information about flight options and pricing.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated basic competence in confirming flight details and listening to the client's preferences. However, they missed opportunities to position themselves as an expert, explore the client's dream outcome, and create urgency for the follow-up call. The call lacked a strong closing structure to ensure the client is fully engaged for the next interaction.
strengths: Confirmed flight details and preferences effectively.
Demonstrated active listening and matched the client's pace.
Provided accurate information about flight options and pricing.
improvements: Position yourself as a travel expert to build credibility.
Ask about the client's dream outcome and budget expectations.
Create urgency and set a specific time for the follow-up call.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Hi, uh, Danny, this is Jerome. I'm calling from Buy Business Class. How are you doing?

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk.

advisor_positioning.evidence: Again, my name is, uh, Jerome. I'll be your personal travel expert, and I'm gonna do my best to get you the best deals possible.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: The agent did not confirm if the client had contacted the company before.

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Uh, Danny, I, I've got to know that you're looking for the, uh, flight tickets from Dallas to, uh, Shenyang. Uh, is that right?

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: Is this your, uh, first time you're traveling to Shenyang, or like you've been there before?

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: And, uh, Danny, how about the dates? Uh, are you flexible with the dates as well, or the dates are fixed?

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about specific comfort or medical needs like lie-flat beds or mobility requirements.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the 'experience' (food/wine/service) or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade for a specific price.

pain_point_identification.evidence: I believe- I'm, I'm not married to American. In fact, I don't really care for American.

budget_inquiry.evidence: The agent did not ask about the client's budget or price expectations.

recap_summary.evidence: So, uh, Danny, as I can see, uh, this flight all the way from Dallas to Shenyang, going with Delta and coming back with the EVA, [PHONE] stops going, [PHONE] stops coming back, and, uh, the dates will be leaving on the March sixteenth and coming back on the August second.

contact_branding.evidence: And then one more thing, like, in future, if there is any kind of changes, cancellation that you are seeking, just let me know, okay? I'll be sending you my direct line number as well.

expert_delay.evidence: The agent did not explain that they need 60-90 minutes to access 'private, unpublished fares.'

urgency_scarcity.evidence: And trust me, even if you'll check the market as well, you're flying the next month, and you see, uh, very limited seats available, and especially for the business class.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for a follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: The agent did not offer the best fare guarantee or the ability to beat any prices.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Okay, just give me one moment. Uh, so I've got your email. That's [EMAIL]. Is that right?

active_listening.evidence: The agent repeated some client responses but occasionally interrupted or did not fully engage with the client's pace.

consultative_expertise.evidence: Even, uh, if I, if I tell you, uh, if the people who are traveling to China, they would, uh, they would prefer EVA Air more than the China Airlines or the other airlines, so.

positive_tone.evidence: The agent maintained a positive tone throughout the call, even during technical difficulties.

authenticity.evidence: The agent engaged in a natural conversation about the client's travel history and preferences.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent's pace was generally appropriate but occasionally rushed through explanations.

answers_questions.evidence: The agent answered questions about TSA PreCheck and flight policies.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent led the call but struggled to manage the email delays and client confusion effectively.

accurate_information.evidence: The agent provided accurate flight details but occasionally caused confusion with multiple options and unclear explanations.

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated strong expertise and maintained a positive tone throughout the call. However, there were missed opportunities to establish urgency, clarify options, and fully engage the client in a structured manner. The call could have been more efficient with better leadership and fewer technical delays.
strengths: Maintained a positive and professional tone.
Demonstrated consultative expertise about airlines and flight options.
Confirmed contact information and provided direct contact details.
improvements: Ask for permission to proceed at the start of the call.
Clarify and simplify flight options to avoid client confusion.
Establish a clear follow-up plan with a specific time.

enthusiastic_tone.evidence: Agent: Hi, Linda, this is Marshall. I'm calling from Buy Business Class regarding your flight request to Singapore that you placed on our website.

permission_check.evidence: The agent did not ask if it was a good time to talk at the start of the call.

advisor_positioning.evidence: The agent introduced themselves as 'Marshall' but did not position themselves as a 'Senior Travel Expert' or similar.

marketing_source.evidence: The agent did not ask how the client found out about the company.

split_rule_compliance.evidence: Agent: Sure. I'll be happy to assist you, Linda, but before I proceed, may I ask, have you ever contacted us before?

confirm_flight_details.evidence: Agent: And just to confirm, you're looking for a round trip from, uh, Jackson, Mississippi, to Singapore, right?

dream_outcome_discovery.evidence: The agent did not ask about the occasion or the client's dominant buying motive.

flexibility_value_probing.evidence: Agent: Do you have any flexibility on the dates? Are you flexible? Can you go a day before or day after if it is cheaper?

specific_needs_discovery.evidence: Agent: And do you have any preference regarding the airlines? Client: Uh, no, not really. I can use any airline.

experience_vs_utility.evidence: The agent did not ask if the client values the 'experience' or just the seat.

upsell_testing.evidence: The agent did not test the budget by offering a potential First Class upgrade.

pain_point_identification.evidence: Client: And I don't want to fly to Europe 'cause that's a long route. Preferably fly to either Japan or Hong Kong or Korea.

budget_inquiry.evidence: Agent: And how much is it, ma'am? Client: Uh, it's total ten.

recap_summary.evidence: Agent: If this is confirmed, Linda, can you please reply to this email, 'I confirm,' or, 'I acknowledge'?

contact_branding.evidence: Agent: Can you also write down my phone number in case if you need any assistance in future or, uh, regarding this ticket or any other ticket, you can just give me a call directly.

expert_delay.evidence: The agent did not explain that they need 60-90 minutes to access 'private, unpublished fares.'

urgency_scarcity.evidence: Agent: And only two seats are available at this point.

hard_appointment.evidence: The agent did not suggest a specific time for a follow-up call.

fare_guarantee_offer.evidence: Agent: I checked online as well, ma'am, but, uh, I think this seems to be an exclusive fare.

contact_info_confirm.evidence: Agent: Can you check if you received it? Client: Uh, do you have my email on you? Okay.

active_listening.evidence: The agent repeated and confirmed details but occasionally interrupted the client.

consultative_expertise.evidence: The agent provided some expertise but did not fully demonstrate consultative authority.

positive_tone.evidence: The agent maintained a polite tone but lacked consistent positivity and energy.

authenticity.evidence: Agent: Ma'am, let me check. Just a moment.

pace_matching.evidence: The agent's pace was generally appropriate but occasionally rushed.

answers_questions.evidence: Agent: Ticket protection will give you extra benefit, ma'am, if you want a refund in case of hospitalization.

call_leadership.evidence: The agent led the call but allowed excessive silences and lacked control during some parts.

accurate_information.evidence: Agent: 'Ticket changes are permitted without any airline penalty or processing fee.'

evaluations.overall_assessment: The agent demonstrated a basic understanding of the client's needs and provided accurate information. However, the call lacked energy, structure, and advanced techniques to build trust and urgency. Excessive silences and missed opportunities to position expertise weakened the overall impact.
strengths: Confirmed flight details accurately.
Addressed client concerns about legitimacy and ticket flexibility.
Provided accurate information about ticket terms and conditions.
improvements: Use a more enthusiastic and confident tone to establish authority.
Ask about the client's dream outcome or occasion to build rapport.
Proactively suggest a follow-up time and explain the need for research time.
critical_gaps: MULTI_AGENT: 2 agents detected in call — Marshall, Calling. QA score may mix multiple agents' performance.
